plan: Trim SPY to meet the 20% single-name concentration limit, reducing overall exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad index exposure.
Trim QQQ to 15% to rebalance broad index holdings.
Buy AAPL at 10% to add a top-quality, high-score name to the portfolio.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall equity exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a realistic target of ~1.5%/month. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, favoring stability. Expect lower drawdowns but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to adhere to the single-name concentration limit.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure and overall risk.
Trim QQQ to 15% and SMH to 10% to reduce broad index and sector concentration.
Buy AVGO at 10% and AAPL at 10% to add high-quality tech names from the scanner.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer, balancing growth with risk management.
Ensure total adds do not exceed total trims plus the cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan prioritizes risk-first growth, targeting ~2.5%/month with moderate drawdowns. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index and quality tech. Expect a balance of growth and stability.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with the single-name concentration limit.
Add SOXL to 10% to leverage semiconductor upside, within combined ETF limits.
Sell QQQI to reallocate capital towards higher growth potential.
Increase SMH to 15% and CRWD to 15% to concentrate in tech and AI sectors.
Buy AVGO at 15% and AAPL at 10% to add high-scoring scanner setups.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer (~5%), leaning into leverage and concentration for growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for stretch growth, targeting ~4%/month, by leveraging high-scoring setups and increasing concentration. Be aware this strategy significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and strict stop discipline is critical to manage rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and reallocate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
Add QQQI to 40% to maximize income and covered-call exposure.
Sell QQQ, SMH, and CRWD to reduce growth-oriented and high-beta single-name exposure.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth potential.
Keep a cash buffer for stability.
income.read: This income plan focuses on parking cash for yield, targeting ~1%/month through monthly distributions. It significantly cuts leverage and high-beta names for stability. While prioritizing low drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs.
